title: Arden Lyn
content:

{{Quote|I awoke to find that twenty-five thousand years had gone by—all I knew and all I loved curdled into history then dismissed as myth. Not even dust remains of those who were dear to me. I have outlived planets, and the very constellations are scrambled and strange. I am alone in a way none of you can possibly imagine.|Arden Lyn|<ref name="EGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>}}
'''Arden Lyn''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] and master of [[Teräs Käsi/Legends|''Teräs Käsi'']] who belonged to the [[Followers of Palawa]]. In [[24,500 BBY]], this [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] became the lover of [[Xendor]] and, upon the advent of the [[First Great Schism]], his right hand in the [[Legions of Lettow]]. When Xendor was [[Death/Legends|killed]] during the conflict, Lyn ascended to leadership of the Legions of Lettow until [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Awdrysta Pina]] [[Duel on Irkalla|confronted her]] on [[Irkalla]]. Lyn killed Pina with a powerful [[Kashi Mer talisman]], but the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Master [[Morichro/Legends|stopped Lyn's heart]] before being destroyed, sending the martial artist into a [[Hibernation trance/Legends|Force trance]].

24,496 years later, in [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]], at the end of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], Lyn was reawakened by three of [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]'s [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitors]]: [[Antinnis Tremayne]], [[Ameesa Darys]], and [[Grand Inquisitor/Legends|Grand Inquisitor]] [[Laddinare Torbin]]. After fighting off Tremayne and killing Darys, Lyn was [[Cho sun/Legends|maimed]] by Torbin, who spared her life so that she could serve Sidious as an [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]]. Lyn did so for a while, spurred on by the Sidious's promise that he would resurrect Xendor from the dead. When she finally came to accept that Sidious had been lying, she turned rogue, and aided [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]] in his attempt to [[Battle of Imperial Center|abduct]] Sidious. The endeavor failed, and Lyn was forced to flee. Sidious eventually hunted down and killed Lyn.

==Biography==
===First Great Schism===
[[File:Arden_Lyn_and_Xendor.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Arden Lyn and Xendor]]
A [[Followers of Palawa|Follower of Palawa]]<ref name="tep" /> born during the [[Expansionist Period/Legends|early days]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],<ref name="Miniatures">{{SWMiniCite|set=Masters of the Force|pack=Arden Lyn}}</ref> the [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] Arden Lyn was a master of the [[Teräs Käsi/Legends|''Teräs Käsi'']] martial art.<ref name="tep" /> The Followers of Palawa were noted for mastering a technique for falling into a deep [[Hibernation trance/Legends|Force trance]], allowing them to sleep for decades without aging. In [[24,500 BBY]],<ref name="jvs" /> after her investment as a [[Steel Hand of Palawa]], she went to the world of [[Pelgrin]] to learn her fortune from [[The Oracle (Pelgrin)|the Oracle]]. It told her that she would have to go to [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], the home of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], where she would meet a man with a clever mind and a fair face, and learn from him. When she arrived, she was welcomed by the Jedi as a fellow student of the Force, and joined them. Lyn however, was dissatisfied with the Order embracing the philosophy of the [[Caamasi/Legends|Caamasi]] people. While studying on Ossus, she met [[Xendor]], a [[Kashi Mer Dynasty|Kashi Mer]] [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] who wanted to learn about ''Teräs Käsi'', and they secretly conversed, each teaching the other. They became lovers. At first, Xendor merely wanted to set up another academy to study other Force traditions.<ref name="EGTW" />

When the hierarchs refused, he and Lyn left the Order and founded their [[Legionnaire Academy|own academy]] on [[Lettow]], with other Jedi<ref name="EGTW" /> and Followers of Palawa<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> later accompanying them. Relations between the Order and Xendor and his disciples, who called themselves the [[Legions of Lettow]], deteriorated, kicking off a war. The Jedi and the Legions of Lettow formed armies, with Xendor being the General and leader of the Legions, and Lyn his right hand.<ref name="EGTW" /> Together, the two of them wreaked havoc during the [[First Great Schism]] as they waged war against the Jedi Order.<ref name="tep" /> During the [[Battle of Columus]], Xendor was slain by<ref name="EGTW" /> [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]]<ref name="Force">[[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook]]</ref> [[Awdrysta Pina]].<ref name="EGTW" />

Following Xendor's [[Death/Legends|death]] on [[Columus/Legends|Columus]], Lyn ascended to the leadership of the Legions of Lettow.<ref name="jvs" /> However, she knew that there was no longer a chance of victory, so they fled. Subsequently, she was tracked down by Pina, who followed her to [[Irkalla]]. They [[Duel on Irkalla|dueled]] until Pina disarmed Lyn of her sword, and she used a powerful [[Kashi Mer talisman]] that Xendor gave her, to focus all her rage, her grief and her love of Xendor to shatter Pina's katana blade, and drive its pieces into his body, fatally wounding him, bringing joy to her.<ref name="EGTW" /> However, Pina used the technique of [[Morichro/Legends|''morichro'']] to stop Lyn's [[Heart/Legends|heart]] before he died.<ref name="tep" />

===Reawakening===
[[File:ArdenLyn_SWGamer.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Arden Lyn, Emperor's Hand]]
Due to her possession of the Kashi Mer talisman<ref name="tep" /> and possibly with the help of [[Palawa hibernation trance|Palawan techniques]],<ref name="jvs" /> Lyn survived Pina's attack by falling into a Force trance.<ref name="tep" /> At some point, an unidentified party discovered Lyn's body, and constructed a [[Arden Lyn's tomb|tomb]], in which it was interred.<ref name="EGTW" /> 

Approximately 24,496 years later, in [[4 BBY/Legends|4 BBY]],<ref name="EGTW" /> during the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] at the end of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], her decomposed body was located by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Jedi hunter/Legends|Jedi hunters]], the [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitors]]<ref name="tep" /><ref name="negtc">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> [[Antinnis Tremayne]], [[Ameesa Darys]], and [[Grand Inquisitor/Legends|Grand Inquisitor]] [[Laddinare Torbin]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. They located the Kashi Mer talisman near her body, and as they brought the artifact into Lyn's proximity she was reawakened, her body rejuvenated. Lyn believed the Inquisitors to be the ones who had killed Xendor many years prior, so she attacked them.<ref name="tep" />

She severely injured Tremayne and killed Darys before Torbin [[Cho sun/Legends|cleaved off]] her right [[Limb/Legends|arm]]. Defeated, Lyn was at the Inquisitors' mercy, but although Tremayne wanted her killed, Torbin believed that the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] might find some use for her rare martial-arts skills.<ref name="tep" /> He was also impressed by her use of the dark side of the Force,<ref name="cswe" /> so he spared Lyn's life.<ref name="tep" />

Palpatine, who was also the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Sidious, did indeed have great use for Lyn, and promised to bring Xendor's spirit back from the [[Netherworld of the Force/Legends|netherworld]] of [[Chaos/Legends|Chaos]] if she chose to serve him. She agreed, and Sidious had her severed arm replaced with that of an ancient [[Post-Manderon period|pre–Old Sith Wars era]] [[Juggernaut war droid]].<ref name="tep" /><ref name="negtd">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref> For several years, Lyn served as the [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]], believing that she was the only one to hold such a title. In reality, Sidious employed many other [[Dark side adept/Legends|dark side adepts]] as "Hands."<ref name="nec" /> Lyn also operated under the authority of Sidious' [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]],<ref name="psg">[[Star Wars: Masters of Teräs Käsi: Prima's Official Hints and Tips|''Star Wars: Masters of Teräs Käsi'': Prima's Official Hints and Tips]]</ref> and acted as his right-hand woman in matters pertaining to ''Teräs Käsi''.<ref name="Insider36">{{InsiderCite|36|Be the Jedi in Three New LucasArts Games}}</ref>

Meanwhile, Lyn served the Empire by training Sidious' [[Byss Mage]]s and [[Imperial Sovereign Protector|Sovereign Protectors]],<ref name="tep" /> and at some stage began working as [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]]'s agent on [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]].<ref name="ww">{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}}</ref> Eventually, she came to realize that the Emperor had no intention of coming through on his promise to resurrect Xendor. As a result, following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] at [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]], when Sidious tasked her with tracking down and destroying key figureheads in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], Lyn used her new freedom to turn rogue.<ref name="tep" />

===The Zaarin insurrection===
[[File:Arden_Lyn_and_Zaarin.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Arden Lyn and Demetrius Zaarin]]
Lyn enlisted students to her cause, training them with the intent of eventually turning them against the Emperor.<ref name="tep" /> At least three were identified. One of her students was the notorious [[Gaderffii/Legends|gaderffii]]-wielding [[KkH'Oar'Rrhr|Hoar]], a [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] from [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] who was acclaimed as the greatest fighter of his people as well as a member of the Tusken splinter group [[Grave Tuskens]]. She instructed him in the arts of ''Teräs Käsi'', and he in turn fought to defeat her enemies, hoping to gain her favor. Also amongst her recruits were the [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] [[Thok (Gamorrean guard)/Legends|Thok]], whose competitive nature was appealed to when faced with the prospect of battling members of the Alliance, and when he, per Gamorr tradition, served her after she bested him in a close battle after she freed him from prison. Her third student was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] known only as [[17786]], whose identity was largely a mystery other than being the offspring of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Nial Declann]].<ref name="Barely Tolerable 2" />

Among Lyn's targets was the Jedi-in-training [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the son of Darth Vader, who was at that time struggling against the temptations of the dark side. Lyn sought to capitalize on Skywalker's inner conflict while planning to destroy him.<ref name="psg" /> When the Followers of Palawa, still in existence during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], were attacked on [[Bunduki/Legends|Bunduki]] by an Imperial detachment led by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Yun]], Lyn entered the fray, attacking both the Palawan monks, with whom she had some old scores to settle, and the Imperial forces.<ref name="dfs">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050512b|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 3: Pride of the Dark Side}}</ref> Secretly, however, she had intended to use her students to overthrow Sidious. However, her plans failed before they could come to fruition, as Mara Jade, another Emperor's Hand, reported on her activities to the Emperor, forcing her alongside Thok and 17786 to go into hiding (Hoar had left the group by this time after he disobeyed a direct order to kill Skywalker).<ref name="Barely Tolerable 2" />

Lyn was later contacted by Grand Admiral Zaarin, himself planning a coup against the Emperor, who hoped that the master of ''Teräs Käsi'' would catch the Emperor off-guard during his planned coup of the Empire.<ref name="nec" /> Over Imperial Center, Zaarin's forces disabled the Emperor's [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Majestic (Imperial-class)|''Majestic'']], which was then boarded.<ref name="tief">[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]</ref> Lyn used her [[Force power/Legends|powers]] to abduct the Emperor,<ref name="ww" /> although the [[Zaarin coup forces|conspirators]]' gambit was eventually [[Battle of Imperial Center|foiled]] due to the arrival of Imperial forces,<ref name="negtc" /> amongst them Inquisitor Tremayne.<ref name="cswe" /> With the arrival of [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] and his flagship, the [[Vanguard (Imperial-class)|''Vanguard'']], Zaarin's forces were forced to flee Imperial Center.<ref name="tief" /> As Lyn fled in the wake of the botched coup, she was pursued by Tremayne, who was still resentful over his loss to her years prior. She began focusing on finding the Kashi Mer talisman that had afforded her earlier success, and had allowed her to cheat death.<ref name="tep" />

===Exile and death===
At some time before or after Sidious' death during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Lyn met and fell in love with the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Durrei]], who was part of a "[[Restored Empire]]" under [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Ennix Devian]].<ref name="The Imperial Warlords">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-2|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref> In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Durrei had been tasked with reclaiming the Kashi Mer relic.<ref name="The Imperial Warlords" /><ref name="Relic">{{JournalCite|6|Relic (article)}}</ref> Once the talisman was in his possession, Devian no longer saw any use for Durrei. He betrayed him and Lyn to his old sparring partner and Lyn's nemesis, Antinnis Tremayne.<ref name="The Imperial Warlords" />

A year later, Darth Sidious was resurrected through the use of [[Cloning/Legends|clones]].<ref name="jvs" /> The reborn Dark Lord of the Sith took revenge on Lyn,<ref name="nec" /> killing her conclusively in [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]].<ref name="jvs" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Arden_Lyn_JTL.png|thumb|right|300px|Lyn was a master martial artist.]]
A destructive individual, Lyn and her lover Xendor wreaked havoc and tumult throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during the First Great Schism. After her reawakening by the Inquisitors, Lyn's mind was highly unstable, and she had been driven insane. When Darth Sidious recruited her to his cause, he appealed to her love for the long-dead Xendor, promising to bring him back to life. Although initially swayed by Sidious's words, Lyn eventually came to the realization that the Emperor had tricked her, and at the first opportunity, turned on him.<ref name="tep" /> Lyn was also adept at recruiting students to her cause, as she demonstrated after turning rogue.<ref name="Barely Tolerable 2" /> When faced with a specific opponent to hunt down and confront, Lyn looked for weaknesses to exploit.<ref name="psg" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|You are a pitiful fighter.|Arden Lyn|<ref name="mtk" />}}
[[File:ArdenLyn-TSS.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Arden Lyn in action]]
As a master of ''Teräs Käsi'', Lyn wielded the powers of the Force with malevolence. Her powers were heightened through the use of a Kashi Mer talisman, which allowed her to destroy a Jedi Master. Her skills served her well when faced with Force-adept opponents, such as the Inquisitors, as she was able to fight off three of them at once, even killing Inquisitor Darys in the process.<ref name="tep" /> In addition to her ''Teräs Käsi'' skills, Lyn was able to harness sound energy, conjuring powerful sonic shock waves.<ref name="tep" /><ref name="mtk" /> Lyn often used her Force powers and martial arts skills in conjunction with her [[Cybernetics/Legends|prosthetic]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] arm, which she could use to shoot bolts of energy.<ref name="mtk" /> She was apparently skilled enough to, along with her students Thok and 17786, subdue and capture Darth Sidious himself.<ref name="dd">"[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]"</ref>

Lyn's other powers included [[Battlemind]], [[Force valor]], [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force grip and push]], and [[Force healing/Legends|Force healing]]. Lyn could also speak, read, and write [[Bunduki (language)|Bunduki]], as well as [[Old Galactic Standard]]. She could also repair items and disable various devices.<ref name="tep" /> It was suggested by Jedi Master [[Tionne Solusar]], when writing about Lyn during the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], that Lyn's study of Palawan trance techniques allowed her to survive Master Pina's ''morichro'' attack at the end of the First Great Schism.<ref name="jvs" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|With this game, aside from Leia there was definitely a drive to get another strong female character. That was part of getting Arden Lyn off the ground.|[[Camela Boswell]], [[LucasArts]] employee, on the creation of Arden Lyn for ''Masters of Teräs Käsi''|<ref name="Insider36" />}}
[[File:ArdenLynProductionArt.jpg|thumb|right|125px|Production art of Arden Lyn, depicting her without her prosthetic arm]]
Arden Lyn first appeared in the [[1997]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Masters of Teräs Käsi]]''. Of the playable combatants in the game, she was one of the two original characters, alongside Hoar. The character of Lyn came about when the game developers felt that there was a need to introduce a strong female character in ''Masters of Teräs Käsi''.<ref name="Insider36" /> She was voiced in-game by [[Edie Mirman]]. Some of Arden Lyn's story was introduced in ''[[Star Wars: Masters of Teräs Käsi: Prima's Official Hints and Tips|Masters of Teräs Käsi: Prima's Official Hints and Tips]]'', and this was in turn expounded upon in ''[[The Emperor's Pawns]]'', written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Juan Schwartz]] with [[Pablo Hidalgo]]. Although the statements in the strategy guide had been vague, Peña decided to establish that Lyn originated in the early years of the Jedi, and placed the character's early life 25,000 years before the events of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' films. In deepening her character, Peña looked to her name, and believed that it resembled the words "harden" and "ardor." Combining the two, he decided to make Lyn a "passionate woman with a hard heart." The article also tied Lyn's story into that of the computer game [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], by linking her to Admiral Zaarin's coup attempt. Furthermore, Peña touched on the content of ''Masters of Teräs Käsi'' itself, which features iconic ''Star Wars'' characters, and Lyn, seemingly facing off in a fighting tournament across disparate worlds. This was explained as being part of Lyn's mission to hunt down Alliance figureheads during the war.<ref name="blog">{{Blog|abelgpena|84|The Emperor's Pawns Endnotes, Part 4: Mistress of Teräs Käsi}}</ref>

The original manuscript for Peña and [[Daniel Wallace]]'s ''[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]'', entitled ''[[Dirty Dozen: The Imperial Grand Admirals]]'', stated that Lyn was aided in her abduction of Palpatine by her pupils, and that Zaarin's overall plan included killing the Emperor, which the conspirators were close to doing before being stopped. These references were cut from the final article, although Lyn was still mentioned.<ref name="dd" />

''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' states that Lyn was at first confronted by Tremayne, and then later hunted down by Torbin, which clashes with the events described and detailed in ''The Emperor's Pawns''. 

''Masters of Teräs Käsi'' also contains a non-[[canon]] ending for Lyn, in which she defeats Darth Vader, standing triumphantly over his body.

Art of Arden Lyn included with ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' and ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' shows her with blue hair, as does art of the First Great Schism included with [[The Journal of Master Gnost-Dural (real-life book)|''The Journal of Master Gnost-Dural'']] and ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]'', but her hair is black in all other appearances.
